The latest evidence slightly strengthens the current top1 because yet another microscopic inspection of the same baseline bundle failed to expose torsion-sensitive, state-specific, or new raw observables that would support a twisted charge-transfer picture. The top2 challenger ICT is essentially unchanged at a low level: it remains blocked by capability but chemically disfavored by the donor-acceptor-free all-hydrocarbon structure. An additional still-credible hypothesis, TICT, remains in coverage debt even though it is substantively weaker than ICT, because it has not yet been moved to an allowed terminal ledger status. Therefore the case must remain in portfolio screening, not pairwise contraction. The key remaining discriminator is not broad mechanism chemistry but explicit internal capability closure for TICT on the stalled parse-only route. Given that this is round 20 of 20, the best next action is one final bounded microscopic request targeted solely at obtaining explicit capability-closure wording for TICT.
